nodes.label: Workflow 1: Lead Instant Response (Claude AI classification + WhatsApp < 60s)
Workflow 2: Booking Confirmation Engine (fires on paid booking INSERT)
Paystack Webhook Verification (HMAC-SHA512 on raw body)
Rate Limiting Rules (Upstash Redis per-route limits)
Project Folder Structure (src/app, src/components, src/lib, supabase/functions)
Build Status Tracker (Done vs To Build sessions A–RR)
Creative North Star: The Digital Concierge
Design Rule: No-Line Rule (no 1px solid borders — use color shifts)
Typography: Satisfy Script (Human Touch — sparingly for Nigerian cultural warmth)
Design Pattern: Tonal Layering (depth via surface token stacking, not drop shadows)
Design Pattern: Ghost Border (outline-variant at 15% opacity for accessibility)
Design Constraint: Mobile-First Layout (min 48px touch targets)
Rationale: No borders maintain Physical Layering illusion
Rationale: Glassmorphism on navbars lets travel imagery bleed through, maintaining sense of place
Rationale: Satisfy script font resonates with vibrant, expressive Nigerian culture
Rationale: Single database/admin/login — product company approach, not bolted-on tools
Rationale: Claude AI NOT used for confirmations/reminders — only for classification and personalisation
Rationale: Duffel never called from browser — always server-side only

visa_documents > file_url text NOT NULL ← Cloudinary secure URL
Workflow 1: Claude API classifies lead category
fires on booking INSERT with payment_status=paid
customers who started booking form but didn't pay
Files upload directly to Cloudinary from browser
raw token sent via Resend email only
tsvector on: tours (title, destination, description, highlights)
Don't use 1px solid black or grey borders. They break the Physical Layering illusion.
Glassmorphism: Use surface_container_lowest with 70% opacity... allow travel imagery to bleed through
Media upload step of tour creation
Mobile-first — build for 375px, then scale up
Next.js App Router: page.tsx, layout.tsx, route.ts
Never call Duffel from browser — always server-side
Both patterns implement the same depth-without-borders principle
Both are depth-without-shadow techniques, glassmorphism is a special case of tonal layering
